spec_match.rationale: The studio constructs correct, valid CSS comma-separated @container syntax matching the W3C CSSContain 3 drafts.

interactive.rationale: Excellent interface. Users can add named container clauses, toggle operator directions, set trigger values, choose the triggered style property, copy code blocks, and drag sliders.

edge_cases.rationale: The generated CSS is now injected into <style id="dynamic-style">, the preview DOM receives matching container classes, and JS inline styling is limited to the surfaced fallback path for browsers that drop comma-separated @container rules.

design_system.rationale: Clean, professional panel layout with compact controls, high contrast borders, and copy-to-clipboard functionality.

summary: Verified with chrome-devtools-mcp in Chrome/148.0.0.0 at http://localhost:3000/v150/comma-separated-container-queries/authoring-studio/. Before the fix, #dynamic-style stayed empty and the preview only mutated #component-card inline styles. After the fix, #dynamic-style is populated with the generated comma-separated @container rule and the preview host gets the matching wrapper classes. Chrome 148 parses single @container rules but drops comma-separated ones, so the page correctly surfaced `JS fallback preview` and limited inline writes to that fallback path. Width checks covered 500px match, 501px no match, 280px match, add/remove query, sanitized `bad name 123` -> `bad-name-123`, property switching through font-size/padding/display/background/border without stale styles, and the Copy button. The /conformance/ route ran with 2 pass / 5 fail in Chrome 148, matching the browser's current lack of comma-query support. Console had no warnings/errors and network requests succeeded.
